text.title: language-framework-guide Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for language-framework-guide as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent picks the stack the prompt mentioned most recently, not the one that fits the requirements.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Recency bias in LLMs. Randomize the option order in the prompt and anchor the agent to read requirements first.

failure-mode.description: Agent recommends last year's defaults (Create React App, webpack, black+isort) instead of current best-practice (Vite, Bun, ruff).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Training cutoffs lag. Anchor prompts with the current year and use this methodology as the authoritative current mapping.

failure-mode.description: Agent copies format/test commands verbatim into projects with a different directory layout, causing path errors.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Commands assume src/ for Python and top-level for JS. Always adapt to the actual project structure before running.

failure-mode.description: Agent applies this guide to ML, embedded, blockchain, or game domains.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The table covers web/API stacks only. Use domain-specific methodologies for other areas.
